title: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) and Resource Allocation Guide
summary: Learn how to create a Work Breakdown Structure, break a project into deliverable work packages, assign dates and owners, and allocate resources effectively.
content: Break the project into smaller work packages, map each package to dates and responsible resources. A high‑level WBS example shows phases (e.g., discovery, design, development, testing) and associated team members. Involve the team during planning to surface dependencies and improve accountability.
can_answer: What is a Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)?
How do I allocate resources using a WBS?
How can a WBS improve project planning?
examples: Phase 1 – Discovery (2 weeks) – Lead: Business Analyst, Support: UX Designer.
Phase 2 – Development (8 weeks) – Lead: Front‑end Engineer, QA: Tester.
warnings: A WBS that is too high‑level can hide critical task dependencies.
